The First Passover


(Numbers 9:1–14)

NoteThe Hebrew word can mean lamb or kid; also in verses 4 and 5.
NoteHebrew between the two evenings
NoteLiterally on the two doorposts and on the lintel; similarly in verses 22 and 23
NoteLiterally Gird up your loins

The Feast of Unleavened Bread


(Leviticus 23:4–8; Numbers 28:16–25; Deuteronomy 16:1–8)

NoteThat is, the seven-day period after the Passover during which no leaven may be eaten

The Tenth Plague: Death of the Firstborn

The Exodus Begins

NoteSuccoth means booths or shelters or tabernacles.
